document_meta.recipient_line: All Member Banks, PSP Banks and UPI Applications

document_meta.desired_outcome: Complete document suite for Smart Amount Suggestions

sections.heading: i. Current State
sections.content_instructions:
Describe how the relevant UPI flow works TODAY before this change. Explain the existing mechanism, who the participants are, and how the current process operates. Minimum 2 paragraphs. Business language only — no XML tags or API field names.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State: Today a UPI payment is initiated by the payer app where the user manually enters the transaction amount. The amount is sent in the ReqPay API. For amounts > INR 15,000 a UPI PIN must be supplied; for lower amounts a PIN is required only for the first transaction (seqNum=1). The flow proceeds through authentication (ReqAuthDetails/RespAuthDetail) and debit/credit APIs without any UI‑level amount suggestion.
Limitations / Pain Points: Manual amount entry leads to longer transaction times and higher probability of input errors. Users also lack contextual guidance based on past payment patterns, which can cause repeated mistakes especially for frequent contacts or merchants.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required] New API message** – We propose to introduce a **GetSmartAmountSuggestions** request/response pair that the payer PSP calls (after the user selects a contact/merchant) to retrieve a ranked list of historic amounts. The response would include an array of suggestion objects (amount, frequency, last‑used‑date). Confirm this API is acceptable, or specify an alternative name/structure.
A: ok proceed

Q: **[Required] Schema field changes** – To carry the suggestion selection back to NPCI we suggest adding two optional fields to the existing **ReqPay** payload:  
   * **suggestionId** (String, ≤ 20) – a UUID generated by the GetSmartAmountSuggestions response to identify the chosen suggestion.  
   * **suggestionSource** (String, values = “CONTACT”, “MERCHANT”) – indicates whether the suggestion originates from a peer‑to‑peer contact or a merchant. Confirm these fields (or propose different names/values) for inclusion in the ReqPay schema.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Reward types** – The feature description does not mention any incentive, but many PSPs may wish to offer **cash‑back** or **loyalty points** for using suggested amounts. We assume **no reward** will be attached unless explicitly defined. Confirm whether any reward type (e.g., “CASHBACK”, “POINTS”) should be supported, or state that rewards are out of scope.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok
sections.prompt_instruction: Write 2 paragraphs: (1) the existing process and participants, (2) how the current system handles the relevant scenario.

sections.heading: ii. Limitations / Challenges
sections.content_instructions:
Explain why the current state is insufficient. Cover security gaps, friction points, scalability concerns, or regulatory gaps. Use bullet points or numbered items for each limitation. Minimum 1 paragraph + 4 bullet points.

sections.prompt_instruction: Write 2 paragraphs: (1) the business case and regulatory context, (2) the expected benefits and why this approach was chosen over alternatives.

sections.heading: i. Product Description
sections.content_instructions:
Provide a high-level description of what this feature/product does and what business outcome it achieves. Name the affected participants and the scope of changes. Business language only. Minimum 2 paragraphs.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State (use in Background section):
  Today a UPI payment is initiated by the payer app where the user manually enters the transaction amount. The amount is sent in the ReqPay API. For amounts > INR 15,000 a UPI PIN must be supplied; for lower amounts a PIN is required only for the first transaction (seqNum=1). The flow proceeds through authentication (ReqAuthDetails/RespAuthDetail) and debit/credit APIs without any UI‑level amount suggestion.
Limitations (use in Background section):
  Manual amount entry leads to longer transaction times and higher probability of input errors. Users also lack contextual guidance based on past payment patterns, which can cause repeated mistakes especially for frequent contacts or merchants.
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The PSP app shall display a list of suggested amounts derived from the payer's historical transactions for the selected contact or merchant.
  FR-02: The suggested amount list shall be editable, allowing the user to modify the amount before proceeding.
  FR-03: Upon user selection or edit, the final amount shall be populated into the ReqPay request field "amount" exactly as defined in NPCI specifications.
  FR-04: The system shall enforce existing PIN rules – if the final amount >15000, the UPI PIN entry screen must be presented before ReqPay is sent.
  FR-05: The suggestion engine shall not alter any mandatory request fields (VPA, initiationMode, purposeCode, UMN) defined by NPCI.
  FR-06: The PSP must log the chosen suggested amount and any user edits for audit and dispute handling as per UDIR guidelines.
Dispute Framework:
  Standard UDIR dispute and chargeback processes apply unchanged; no new dispute types are introduced by the Smart Amount Suggestions feature.
Participant Obligations:
  PSP Banks: must implement all mandatory APIs (ReqPay, ReqAuthDetails, ReqDebit, ReqCredit, ReqTxnConfirmation).; must validate amount against PIN rules and enforce PIN entry when required.
  Issuer Banks: must respond to debit/credit requests (ReqDebit/ReqCredit) with appropriate status codes.; must support real‑time settlement as per NPCI settlement cycles.
  NPCI: will route authentication and debit/credit messages between PSPs.; will validate mandatory fields (VPA, amount, purposeCode) and enforce PIN thresholds.

Flow Sequence (business language):
  ["Step 1: Payer PSP \u2014 initiates ReqPay with type Pay, amount, VPA and optional voucher details.", "Step 2: NPCI \u2014 forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP for merchant validation.", "Step 3: Payee PSP \u2014 validates merchant details, returns AC/IFSC in RespAuthDetail.", "Step 4: NPCI \u2014 issues ReqDebit (or ReqCredit for credit\u2011card flows) to debit payer account/voucher.", "Step 5: Payee PSP \u2014 credits merchant account via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation.", "Step 6: Payer PSP \u2014 receives RespTxnConfirmation and notifies user."]
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required] New API message** – We propose to introduce a **GetSmartAmountSuggestions** request/response pair that the payer PSP calls (after the user selects a contact/merchant) to retrieve a ranked list of historic amounts. The response would include an array of suggestion objects (amount, frequency, last‑used‑date). Confirm this API is acceptable, or specify an alternative name/structure.
A: ok proceed

Q: **[Required] Schema field changes** – To carry the suggestion selection back to NPCI we suggest adding two optional fields to the existing **ReqPay** payload:  
   * **suggestionId** (String, ≤ 20) – a UUID generated by the GetSmartAmountSuggestions response to identify the chosen suggestion.  
   * **suggestionSource** (String, values = “CONTACT”, “MERCHANT”) – indicates whether the suggestion originates from a peer‑to‑peer contact or a merchant. Confirm these fields (or propose different names/values) for inclusion in the ReqPay schema.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Reward types** – The feature description does not mention any incentive, but many PSPs may wish to offer **cash‑back** or **loyalty points** for using suggested amounts. We assume **no reward** will be attached unless explicitly defined. Confirm whether any reward type (e.g., “CASHBACK”, “POINTS”) should be supported, or state that rewards are out of scope.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok
sections.prompt_instruction: Write 2-3 paragraphs describing: what the feature does, who it affects, and what problem it solves from a business perspective. No API details.

sections.heading: ii. Product Construct: Setting / Enrollment
sections.content_instructions:
If the feature requires explicit enrollment or opt-in: describe UI placement, consent capture, and the step-by-step enrollment journey with a Roles & Responsibilities table [Step, Activity, Responsible]. If the feature does NOT require a separate enrollment or setting step: write exactly two sentences stating that this section is not applicable for this feature and briefly explain why (e.g., 'This feature does not require a separate enrollment step. Users access it directly from [entry point] without prior registration or opt-in.'). NEVER leave this section blank.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
APIs: [
  {
    "name": "ReqPay",
    "response": "RespPay",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Initiates a payment request with type Pay and voucher/details."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqAuthDetails",
    "response": "RespAuthDetail",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests authentication details from Payee PSP."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqDebit",
    "response": "RespDebit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Debits the payer's account or voucher after authentication."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqCredit",
    "response": "RespCredit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Credits the payee/merchant account after successful debit."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqTxnConfirmation",
    "response": "RespTxnConfirmation",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP / Payee PSP",
    "description": "Confirms transaction status to NPCI."
  }
]
Flow Sequence:
  Step 1: Payer PSP — initiates ReqPay with type Pay, amount, VPA and optional voucher details.
  Step 2: NPCI — forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP for merchant validation.
  Step 3: Payee PSP — validates merchant details, returns AC/IFSC in RespAuthDetail.
  Step 4: NPCI — issues ReqDebit (or ReqCredit for credit‑card flows) to debit payer account/voucher.
  Step 5: Payee PSP — credits merchant account via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation.
  Step 6: Payer PSP — receives RespTxnConfirmation and notifies user.
Auth Method: UPI PIN
Transaction Limit: INR 15,000 per transaction without mandatory PIN (amount >15,000 always requires PIN as per NPCI rules).
User Journey:
  Step 1: User opens PSP app, selects a contact or merchant.
  Step 2: PSP app fetches past transaction amounts for that VPA and shows suggested amounts.
  Step 3: User taps a suggested amount or edits it; the amount field is populated.
  Step 4: If amount >15000, PSP app displays UPI PIN screen; user enters PIN.
  Step 5: PSP app sends ReqPay with populated amount and other mandatory fields.
  Step 6: NPCI routes authentication (ReqAuthDetails/RespAuthDetail) and debit/credit APIs.
  Step 7: PSP receives RespTxnConfirmation and shows success/failure to user.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required] New API message** – We propose to introduce a **GetSmartAmountSuggestions** request/response pair that the payer PSP calls (after the user selects a contact/merchant) to retrieve a ranked list of historic amounts. The response would include an array of suggestion objects (amount, frequency, last‑used‑date). Confirm this API is acceptable, or specify an alternative name/structure.
A: ok proceed

Q: **[Required] Schema field changes** – To carry the suggestion selection back to NPCI we suggest adding two optional fields to the existing **ReqPay** payload:  
   * **suggestionId** (String, ≤ 20) – a UUID generated by the GetSmartAmountSuggestions response to identify the chosen suggestion.  
   * **suggestionSource** (String, values = “CONTACT”, “MERCHANT”) – indicates whether the suggestion originates from a peer‑to‑peer contact or a merchant. Confirm these fields (or propose different names/values) for inclusion in the ReqPay schema.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Reward types** – The feature description does not mention any incentive, but many PSPs may wish to offer **cash‑back** or **loyalty points** for using suggested amounts. We assume **no reward** will be attached unless explicitly defined. Confirm whether any reward type (e.g., “CASHBACK”, “POINTS”) should be supported, or state that rewards are out of scope.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

sections.heading: Dispute Management
sections.content_instructions:
State explicitly whether dispute management changes due to this feature. If unchanged, say so clearly and reference the standard UPI dispute framework. If changed, describe the new liability assignment, SLA, and escalation path. Minimum 2 paragraphs.

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok
sections.prompt_instruction: Start with a declarative statement on whether the dispute process changes. Then describe liability, SLA, and escalation path explicitly.

sections.heading: Functional Requirements
sections.content_instructions:
List all functional requirements for this feature as a table. Table columns: [ID, Requirement, Priority]. IDs: FR-01, FR-02, ... Minimum 8 functional requirement rows. Each requirement is one testable statement. Write a 1-paragraph intro before the table.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The PSP app shall display a list of suggested amounts derived from the payer's historical transactions for the selected contact or merchant.
  FR-02: The suggested amount list shall be editable, allowing the user to modify the amount before proceeding.
  FR-03: Upon user selection or edit, the final amount shall be populated into the ReqPay request field "amount" exactly as defined in NPCI specifications.
  FR-04: The system shall enforce existing PIN rules – if the final amount >15000, the UPI PIN entry screen must be presented before ReqPay is sent.
  FR-05: The suggestion engine shall not alter any mandatory request fields (VPA, initiationMode, purposeCode, UMN) defined by NPCI.
  FR-06: The PSP must log the chosen suggested amount and any user edits for audit and dispute handling as per UDIR guidelines.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required] New API message** – We propose to introduce a **GetSmartAmountSuggestions** request/response pair that the payer PSP calls (after the user selects a contact/merchant) to retrieve a ranked list of historic amounts. The response would include an array of suggestion objects (amount, frequency, last‑used‑date). Confirm this API is acceptable, or specify an alternative name/structure.
A: ok proceed

Q: **[Required] Schema field changes** – To carry the suggestion selection back to NPCI we suggest adding two optional fields to the existing **ReqPay** payload:  
   * **suggestionId** (String, ≤ 20) – a UUID generated by the GetSmartAmountSuggestions response to identify the chosen suggestion.  
   * **suggestionSource** (String, values = “CONTACT”, “MERCHANT”) – indicates whether the suggestion originates from a peer‑to‑peer contact or a merchant. Confirm these fields (or propose different names/values) for inclusion in the ReqPay schema.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Reward types** – The feature description does not mention any incentive, but many PSPs may wish to offer **cash‑back** or **loyalty points** for using suggested amounts. We assume **no reward** will be attached unless explicitly defined. Confirm whether any reward type (e.g., “CASHBACK”, “POINTS”) should be supported, or state that rewards are out of scope.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok

sections.heading: A. NPCI (UPI Platform & CL) — Setting / Schema / Registration Changes
sections.content_instructions:
List all NPCI platform, Common Library (CL), and schema changes required for the enrollment/setting flow. Number each requirement. Cover: new API messages, changed schema fields, CL version changes, configuration flags. Minimum 2 paragraphs + ≥ 4 numbered requirements.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
User Journey:
  Step 1: User opens PSP app, selects a contact or merchant.
  Step 2: PSP app fetches past transaction amounts for that VPA and shows suggested amounts.
  Step 3: User taps a suggested amount or edits it; the amount field is populated.
  Step 4: If amount >15000, PSP app displays UPI PIN screen; user enters PIN.
  Step 5: PSP app sends ReqPay with populated amount and other mandatory fields.
  Step 6: NPCI routes authentication (ReqAuthDetails/RespAuthDetail) and debit/credit APIs.
  Step 7: PSP receives RespTxnConfirmation and shows success/failure to user.
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

sections.heading: B. NPCI (UPI Platform & CL) — Transaction Flow / Processing Changes
sections.content_instructions:
List all NPCI routing, processing, and validation changes for the payment transaction flow. Number each requirement. Cover: refCategory handling, credential validation, switch routing changes, response handling. Minimum 2 paragraphs + ≥ 4 numbered requirements.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE SPECIFICATIONS for this section (NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
APIs: [
  {
    "name": "ReqPay",
    "response": "RespPay",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP",
    "description": "Initiates a payment request with type Pay and voucher/details."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqAuthDetails",
    "response": "RespAuthDetail",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Requests authentication details from Payee PSP."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqDebit",
    "response": "RespDebit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Debits the payer's account or voucher after authentication."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqCredit",
    "response": "RespCredit",
    "initiator": "NPCI",
    "description": "Credits the payee/merchant account after successful debit."
  },
  {
    "name": "ReqTxnConfirmation",
    "response": "RespTxnConfirmation",
    "initiator": "Payer PSP / Payee PSP",
    "description": "Confirms transaction status to NPCI."
  }
]
Flow Sequence:
  Step 1: Payer PSP — initiates ReqPay with type Pay, amount, VPA and optional voucher details.
  Step 2: NPCI — forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP for merchant validation.
  Step 3: Payee PSP — validates merchant details, returns AC/IFSC in RespAuthDetail.
  Step 4: NPCI — issues ReqDebit (or ReqCredit for credit‑card flows) to debit payer account/voucher.
  Step 5: Payee PSP — credits merchant account via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation.
  Step 6: Payer PSP — receives RespTxnConfirmation and notifies user.
Auth Method: UPI PIN
Transaction Limit: INR 15,000 per transaction without mandatory PIN (amount >15,000 always requires PIN as per NPCI rules).
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required] New API message** – We propose to introduce a **GetSmartAmountSuggestions** request/response pair that the payer PSP calls (after the user selects a contact/merchant) to retrieve a ranked list of historic amounts.    * **suggestionSource** (String, values = “CONTACT”, “MERCHANT”) – indicates whether the suggestion originates from a peer‑to‑peer contact or a merchant. Confirm these fields (or propose different names/values) for inclusion in the ReqPay schema.
A: ok

A: ok

sections.diagram_description: NPCI switch processing changes for the new transaction flow

sections.heading: A. UPI App / PSP — App-side Changes
sections.content_instructions:
List all UPI App and PSP changes required on the app side for enrollment, consent capture, and device binding. Number each requirement. Cover: UI changes, CL SDK invocation, local key storage, device check logic. Minimum 2 paragraphs + ≥ 4 numbered requirements.

Feature: Smart Amount Suggestions.

AUTHORITATIVE TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS (derived from NPCI corpus — do NOT invent alternatives):
Current State (use in Background section):
  Today a UPI payment is initiated by the payer app where the user manually enters the transaction amount. The amount is sent in the ReqPay API. For amounts > INR 15,000 a UPI PIN must be supplied; for lower amounts a PIN is required only for the first transaction (seqNum=1). The flow proceeds through authentication (ReqAuthDetails/RespAuthDetail) and debit/credit APIs without any UI‑level amount suggestion.
Limitations (use in Background section):
  Manual amount entry leads to longer transaction times and higher probability of input errors. Users also lack contextual guidance based on past payment patterns, which can cause repeated mistakes especially for frequent contacts or merchants.
Functional Requirements:
  FR-01: The PSP app shall display a list of suggested amounts derived from the payer's historical transactions for the selected contact or merchant.
  FR-02: The suggested amount list shall be editable, allowing the user to modify the amount before proceeding.
  FR-03: Upon user selection or edit, the final amount shall be populated into the ReqPay request field "amount" exactly as defined in NPCI specifications.
  FR-04: The system shall enforce existing PIN rules – if the final amount >15000, the UPI PIN entry screen must be presented before ReqPay is sent.
  FR-05: The suggestion engine shall not alter any mandatory request fields (VPA, initiationMode, purposeCode, UMN) defined by NPCI.
  FR-06: The PSP must log the chosen suggested amount and any user edits for audit and dispute handling as per UDIR guidelines.
Dispute Framework:
  Standard UDIR dispute and chargeback processes apply unchanged; no new dispute types are introduced by the Smart Amount Suggestions feature.
Participant Obligations:
  PSP Banks: must implement all mandatory APIs (ReqPay, ReqAuthDetails, ReqDebit, ReqCredit, ReqTxnConfirmation).; must validate amount against PIN rules and enforce PIN entry when required.
  Issuer Banks: must respond to debit/credit requests (ReqDebit/ReqCredit) with appropriate status codes.; must support real‑time settlement as per NPCI settlement cycles.
  NPCI: will route authentication and debit/credit messages between PSPs.; will validate mandatory fields (VPA, amount, purposeCode) and enforce PIN thresholds.

Flow Sequence (business language):
  ["Step 1: Payer PSP \u2014 initiates ReqPay with type Pay, amount, VPA and optional voucher details.", "Step 2: NPCI \u2014 forwards ReqAuthDetails to Payee PSP for merchant validation.", "Step 3: Payee PSP \u2014 validates merchant details, returns AC/IFSC in RespAuthDetail.", "Step 4: NPCI \u2014 issues ReqDebit (or ReqCredit for credit\u2011card flows) to debit payer account/voucher.", "Step 5: Payee PSP \u2014 credits merchant account via ReqCredit and sends RespTxnConfirmation.", "Step 6: Payer PSP \u2014 receives RespTxnConfirmation and notifies user."]
RULE: Business language only. No XML, no XSD field names.

PM Clarification Answers (authoritative — override assumptions):
Q: **[Required] New API message** – We propose to introduce a **GetSmartAmountSuggestions** request/response pair that the payer PSP calls (after the user selects a contact/merchant) to retrieve a ranked list of historic amounts. The response would include an array of suggestion objects (amount, frequency, last‑used‑date). Confirm this API is acceptable, or specify an alternative name/structure.
A: ok proceed

Q: **[Required] Schema field changes** – To carry the suggestion selection back to NPCI we suggest adding two optional fields to the existing **ReqPay** payload:  
   * **suggestionId** (String, ≤ 20) – a UUID generated by the GetSmartAmountSuggestions response to identify the chosen suggestion.  
   * **suggestionSource** (String, values = “CONTACT”, “MERCHANT”) – indicates whether the suggestion originates from a peer‑to‑peer contact or a merchant. Confirm these fields (or propose different names/values) for inclusion in the ReqPay schema.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Reward types** – The feature description does not mention any incentive, but many PSPs may wish to offer **cash‑back** or **loyalty points** for using suggested amounts. We assume **no reward** will be attached unless explicitly defined. Confirm whether any reward type (e.g., “CASHBACK”, “POINTS”) should be supported, or state that rewards are out of scope.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok

A: ok

Q: **[Required] Funding model** – Our default assumption is that the **suggestion service (GetSmartAmountSuggestions)** is funded by the participating PSPs (bank‑funded) and does not involve merchant sponsorship. Confirm this funding approach, or indicate if a **merchant‑sponsored** or **third‑party** model is intended.
A: ok

Q: **[Required] Inter‑party settlement** – We assume that transactions initiated via a suggested amount follow the **standard UPI settlement cycle** (real‑time settlement through NPCI’s existing clearinghouse) with no additional settlement steps. Confirm that the existing settlement model applies unchanged, or specify any special settlement arrangement required for this feature.
A: ok
sections.prompt_instruction: Write a short intro, then number each transaction-flow change. Business language.
sections.diagram_description: Issuer bank processing of authenticated debit requests and response flow
